Patch Set 2:

Is the context per NID or per Lustre connection?  Note that there may be multiple client mountpoints ona single NID.

Patch Set 2:

The context is per connection. When Lustre is mounted multiple times on the same client, it displays multiple reverse contexts. For instance, I mounted Lustre twice from client at 192.168.56.206@tcp:

   # lctl get_param mdt.seb-MDT0000.exports.*.srpc_serverctx
   mdt.seb-MDT0000.exports.192.168.56.206@tcp.srpc_serverctx=
   0000000070fac63e: uid 0, ref 1, expire 1702904269(+604187), fl uptodate,cached,, seq 0, win 2048, key 00000000(ref 0), hdl 0xd07670dd0ea5720f:0x472457184706bc5e, mech: sk
   0000000029634ed4: uid 0, ref 1, expire 1702903780(+603698), fl uptodate,cached,, seq 0, win 2048, key 00000000(ref 0), hdl 0xd07670dd0ea5720e:0x472457184706bc5d, mech: sk
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

> For instance, I mounted Lustre twice from client at
 > 192.168.56.206@tcp:
 > 
 > # lctl get_param mdt.seb-MDT0000.exports.*.srpc_serverctx
 > mdt.seb-MDT0000.exports.192.168.56.206@tcp.srpc_serverctx=
 > 0000000070fac63e: uid 0, ref 1, expire 1702904269(+604187), fl
 > uptodate,cached,, seq 0, win 2048, key 00000000(ref 0), hdl
 > 0xd07670dd0ea5720f:0x472457184706bc5e, mech: sk
 > 0000000029634ed4: uid 0, ref 1, expire 1702903780(+603698), fl
 > uptodate,cached,, seq 0, win 2048, key 00000000(ref 0), hdl
 > 0xd07670dd0ea5720e:0x472457184706bc5d, mech: sk

This looks messy to parse. Please modify output slightly so that it is valid YAML. Use "key: value" format for all fields, which means a colon after every key name, remove empty ",,", double quotes around hdl value that contains embedded ':'.

Use https://yaml-online-parser.appspot.com/ to verify format.

Patch Set 3:

The output now looks like this:

   # lctl get_param mdt.testfs-MDT0000.exports.*.srpc_serverctx
   mdt.testfs-MDT0000.exports.192.168.56.206@tcp.srpc_serverctx=ctx: 000000006435d670, uid: 0, ref: 1, expire: 1703076877, delta: 603530, fl: "uptodate,cached", seq: 0, win: 2048, key: 00000000, keyref: 0, hdl: "0x79a391c439b620d8:0x53e379b2809c1d5b", mech: sk

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> The output now looks like this:
> 
>    # lctl get_param mdt.testfs-MDT0000.exports.*.srpc_serverctx
>    mdt.testfs-MDT0000.exports.192.168.56.206@tcp.srpc_serverctx=ctx: 000000006435d670, uid: 0, ref: 1, expire: 1703076877, delta: 603530, fl: "uptodate,cached", seq: 0, win: 2048, key: 00000000, keyref: 0, hdl: "0x79a391c439b620d8:0x53e379b2809c1d5b", mech: sk

-To be easily parseable, that should really be YAML code (to use tool like 'yq'). If we don't do YAML, the comma are rather painful for shell parsing. You need { ... }  to make that YAML compatible. 
- the context id could be also be a key: 

   - 000000006435d670: { uid: 0, ref: 1, ... }

- mech should be either using quotes, or changed, because for krb, this is "krb5 (aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96)"

see: https://yaml-online-parser.appspot.com/?yaml=%7B+ctx%3A+000000006435d670%2C+uid%3A+0%2C+ref%3A+1%2C+expire%3A+1703076877%2C+delta%3A+603530%2C+fl%3A+%22uptodate%2Ccached%22%2C+seq%3A+0%2C+win%3A+2048%2C+key%3A+00000000%2C+keyref%3A+0%2C+hdl%3A+%220x79a391c439b620d8%3A0x53e379b2809c1d5b%22%2C+mech%3A+sk+%7D&type=python

Patch Set 6:

Patchset 6 removes the peer_nid field from the srpc_contexts info displayed on client side. This was confusing because the target can move to a failover NID, and we are already able to identify the peer thanks to the import name in the param path.

parents.subject: LU-17337 osd: ask for more revoke credits

commit.subject: LU-17317 sec: add srpc_serverctx proc file
commit.message:
LU-17317 sec: add srpc_serverctx proc file

GSS srpc contexts for client connections can already be dumped via
proc file <mdc,osc>.*.srpc_contexts.
This patch adds a new proc file to dump server side GSS srpc contexts,
e.g.:
mgs.MGS.gss.srpc_serverctx
mdt.testfs-MDT0000.gss.srpc_serverctx
obdfilter.testfs-OST0000.gss.srpc_serverctx

The GSS context information is dumped as YAML, with one line per
context, like this:
- 0000000013221bdf: { peer_nid: 192.168.56.206@tcp, uid: 0, ctxref: 1,
expire: 1707934985, delta: 3401, flags: [uptodate, cached], seq: 0,
win: 2048, key: 00000000, keyref: 0,
hdl: "0x5ae1a771fd57043:0x65a64972fda4e200",
mech: "krb5 (aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96)" }

Because of this new syntax, sanity-sec test_28 needs to be fixed.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-2
